channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Talk show host and former Navy man Montel Williams co-writes history of USS Intrepid
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A new history of the USS Intrepid will be co-written by a former Navy man otherwise known for his long career as a daytime television host — Montel Williams. “The Sailing of the Intrepid” will be published May 6, Hanover Square Press announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump hurls string of insults at Harris including racist trope against Black people
item.description: Trump signaled he will lean harder into disparaging Harris during the remaining two weeks before Election Day, despite the urging of allies who have repeatedly suggested he should steer clear of personal attacks including references to her race and gender.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fallout from 2023 Hamas attack lingers as Jews worldwide prepare for a holiday meant for joy
item.description: Customarily, Simchat Torah is one of the most joyous days of the Jewish calendar, highlighted by exuberant dancing around a Torah.
item.dc:creator: David Crary, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dodgers phenom Fernando Valenzuela dies at 63
item.description: Fernando Valenzuela, the Mexican-born phenom for the Los Angeles Dodgers who inspired “Fernandomania” while winning the NL Cy Young Award and Rookie of the Year in 1981, has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Illinois jail continues allowing detainees to vote in presidential elections, other local races
item.description: The Cook County Jail in Chicago is teaching civics classes to those detained. They then have a chance to study their options and vote from the jail's chapel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Young women lean Democratic. Now the election may hinge on Harris' ability to run up the score
item.description: This year's presidential election could see a historic gender gap as young women line up behind Democrat Kamala Harris' bid to be the first female president.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein And Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Surfer dies after being struck, impaled by swordfish
item.description: A longtime surfer and travel guide died after being impaled by a swordfish off the coast of Indonesia.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, Fox 5

item.title: Mercedes-Benz crashes through roof of Southern California home after 'elderly' driver loses control
item.description: A woman driving a Mercedes-Benz sedan crashed through the roof of a Southern California home on Sunday evening, leaving a gaping hole in the roof.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missouri woman finds trunk full of stolen campaign signs with help of Apple AirTag
item.description: "I expected to find the AirTag, but not 59 signs. It was kind of like finding a dead body," the Missouri woman said.
item.dc:creator: Parker Padgett, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Capitol Christmas Tree is coming to Utah as part of its 4,000-mile trek -- Here's how it's staying alive
item.description: Here's what you should know about this year's Capitol Christmas Tree — and how the tree will be kept alive during its travels.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Rep. Brandon Williams and challenger John Mannion square off in NY-22 debate
item.description: Debate between candidates in the 22nd Congressional District. Featuring incumbent Brandon Williams and challenger NY State Senator John Mannion.
item.dc:creator: Dave Longley, NewsNation

item.title: Judge rejects Mark Meadows attempt to get White House records for Georgia defense
item.description: A federal judge cast aside former White House chief of staff Mark Meadows's bid for a cache of records from the National Archives meant to aid in his defense against criminal charges in the Georgia election case. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: What Harris and Trump's travel say about state of razor-thin race
item.description: Want to know where the 2024 race is being decided? Just look at where the two candidates are spending their time.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Judge tosses RNC challenge to Michigan voter roll maintenance
item.description: U.S. District Judge Jane Beckering ruled that neither the RNC nor two individual voters who joined the suit had legal standing to bring their case.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Court upholds freedom for woman whose conviction was overturned after 43 years behind bars
item.description: An appellate court in Missouri has ruled that a lower court was right when it decided to overturn the murder conviction of a woman who spent 43 years behind bars for a killing that her attorneys argue was committed by a discredited police officer.
item.dc:creator: Heather Hollingsworth, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge tosses RNC challenge to Michigan voter roll maintenance
item.description: U.S. District Judge Jane Beckering ruled that neither the RNC nor two individual voters who joined the suit had legal standing to bring their case.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: September Border Patrol encounters lowest since the pandemic
item.description: Fewer migrants were apprehended between ports of entry at the southern border in September than in any month since August of 2020, according to new data released by Customs and Border Protection (CBP).
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Scott Perry's Pennsylvania House race now a toss-up
item.description: Perry is in a competitive race with Democrat Janelle Stelson in a battle Cook previously had rated as "lean Republican.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: MLB wants the Tampa Bay Rays to play in the area if hurricane-damaged Tropicana Field is unfit
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Major League Baseball hopes to find the Tampa Bay Rays a temporary home near their fanbase at least for the start of the 2025 season if the damage to Tropicana Field from Hurricane Milton cannot be repaired in time. The translucent fiberglass dome at the ballpark in St.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: These are the 'rattiest' cities in the US, according to Orkin's 2024 report
item.description: Chicago — which was briefly attached to a rat-shaped hole in a sidewalk this year — took the top spot again, marking a decade of ratty dominance.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, Fox 5

item.title: North Carolina county surrounding Asheville overcounted Helene deaths by as many as 30, sheriff says
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — The North Carolina county that is home to Asheville overcounted deaths caused by Helene by as many as 30, according to a statement Tuesday from its sheriff’s office and data from the state, significantly reducing the death toll from the historic storm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Grieving families of seniors killed when a dock collapsed say the tragedy was preventable
item.description: JACKSONVILLE, Fla. (AP) — Regina Brinson heard a crack before the metal walkway gave way beneath her feet, plunging her into the water beneath the state-operated ferry dock on Georgia's Sapelo Island.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Richard N. Winfield, First Amendment lawyer and former AP counsel, dies at 91
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Richard N. Winfield, a leading U.S. First Amendment lawyer who represented The Associated Press for three decades and championed freedom of expression for journalists around the world, died on Tuesday. He was 91.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Georgia teen accused in the Apalachee High School shooting has pleaded not guilty
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A 14-year-old Georgia boy indicted on murder charges in a mass shooting at Apalachee High School pleaded not guilty on Tuesday. Colt Gray's lawyer filed papers entering the plea after Gray was indicted on Thursday. They waived an arraignment hearing that had been scheduled for Nov.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Meta again testing facial recognition software to fight 'celeb bait' scams
item.description: Meta, the parent company of Facebook and Instagram, is testing facial recognition software to combat “celeb bait” scams on its platforms as well as to allow users to more easily regain access to their accounts.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: White House proposes insurers cover cost of over-the-counter birth control
item.description: The White House on Monday announced a proposal that would require insurers to cover the cost of forms of contraception like over-the-counter birth control pills and condoms under the Affordable Care Act.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Patrick Mahomes and Chiefs stay undefeated, beat 49ers 28-18 in Super Bowl rematch
item.description: The formula might look a bit different with the defense and running game carrying the Kansas City Chiefs. But the results are awfully familiar as the two-time defending champions keep knocking off teams — especially the San Francisco 49ers.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cooper scores in his Bills debut and Allen shakes off a sluggish start in 34-10 rout of Titans
item.description: It didn’t take Amari Cooper long to get the hang of Josh Allen and the Buffalo Bills' offense — even when the quarterback was changing plays at the line of scrimmage.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Calmer weather helps contain Oakland, California, fire that forced evacuations
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Firefighting crews took advantage of calmer weather Sunday to increase containment of a small wildfire in Oakland, California, that burned two homes and forced more than 500 people to evacuate.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
